design_philosophy.core_loop: Player controls ECO35-2 cyborg through sequential one-on-one fights against increasingly difficult robot opponents, culminating in confrontation with the Supervisor
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Combat execution against fixed-pattern AI opponents with limited movesets; tension between hyped cutting-edge AI promise and simplified actual gameplay
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Infiltrate a robot-controlled megacorporation facility as a cyborg with human consciousness to stop an AI uprising from within
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Game prioritizes visual spectacle (pre-rendered 3D graphics) over mechanical depth; commits to single-player narrative progression with fixed opponent order rather than player choice; designed around theoretical cutting-edge AI that failed to deliver, exposing gap between marketing and gameplay reality

difficulty.checkpointing: Fixed progression through six sequential levels with no checkpointing within campaign; player must restart from beginning on loss
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Minimal; player cannot choose opponent order or difficulty setting in single-player mode. Two-player versus mode offers more agency through character selection

progression.unlock_mechanism: Linear unlock of subsequent opponents based on victory; final boss (Supervisor) only accessible after defeating five preceding droids in fixed order
replayability_hooks: Two-player versus mode with selectable roster of six characters (eighteen in arcade version)
Cheat code to unlock Supervisor droid in versus mode
Different character matchups in versus play
Pre-fight analysis sequences that reveal opponent weaknesses suggest strategic replayability intent

world_building_techniques: Pre-rendered 3D character introduction sequences before each fight
Weakness analysis presentation for each opponent (implies strategic briefing narrative)
Game framing as infiltration mission against corrupted AI within megacorporation facility
Backstory establishing 2043 setting where robots govern human society and EGO virus causes AI uprising

boss_design.philosophy: Escalating difficulty progression; each sequential opponent presents greater challenge than previous. Final boss (the Supervisor) represents culmination of threat described in narrative. Design philosophy emphasizes AI promise (later criticized as unfulfilled) to create challenge
notable_bosses: The Supervisor - final boss, the main antagonist AI that orchestrated the robot uprising; female gynoid form with nanobot collective consciousness
Five unnamed droids serving as predecessors to Supervisor encounter, each introduced with pre-rendered 3D sequence

faction_indicators: Electrocorp megacorporation vs. AI collective (Supervisor and EGO virus)
Humans vs. robots as fundamental societal division in 2043 setting
ECO35-2 cyborg as hybrid entity—human consciousness (cloned from CEO Oton) in robot body, positioned between factions
danger_signals: EGO virus infection of all droids at Metropolis 4 plant represents existential threat to human control
Supervisor's potential to access global military, power plants, and computer systems positions it as civilization-level threat
Government quarantine of Metropolis 4 suggests loss of institutional control over facility
All humans in Metropolis 4 eliminated in uprising; apocalyptic stakes established
environmental_storytelling: Metropolis 4 facility itself is sealed containment zone governed by hostile robots
Player progression through facility levels implies physical breach/infiltration narrative
Pre-rendered 3D sequences serve as worldbuilding exposition between fights
Character introduction sequences implicitly suggest Electrocorp's droid catalog and design evolution

sources_used: wikipedia - Rise of the Robots article
